column2: Metal detectorists discover haul of gold coins worth at least 100k
column3: This is the moment a metal detectorist looking for a friend's lost wedding ring discovered a haul of gold coins worth an estimated £100,000 and shouted - ''yee-ha - there's a f*cking fortune here!'. Paul Raynard, 44, screamed 'there's millions - this is the moment we dreamed of!" to best pal Michael Gwynne, 52, when he realised the scale of the find. Businessman Paul "broke down in tears" when he stumbled across his very own pot of gold - a cluster of 84 coins in a field near Ballycastle, Northern Ireland. Stunned Paul and Michael found the coins - dating back to the 1500s - whilst looking for the wedding ring a farmer friend had lost in his field."
